title: Add sandboxed bash execution (seccomp/firejail/macOS sandbox-exec)
description:
## Gap

Claude Code wraps every bash execution in a platform sandbox:
- Linux: seccomp or firejail
- macOS: sandbox-exec
- Controlled by SandboxManager (utils/sandbox/sandbox-adapter.js)
- shouldUseSandbox() (tools/BashTool/shouldUseSandbox.ts:130-153) determines per-command whether to sandbox
- Input field dangerouslyDisableSandbox allows bypass when policy allows
- User can configure excludedCommands in settings.sandbox to opt commands out

OpenClaudia has no sandbox. Bash runs directly via std::process::Command. The only isolation is:
- process_group(0) for clean SIGTERM delivery (src/tools/bash/mod.rs:82)
- apply_env_scrub to strip credential env vars (src/tools/bash/policy.rs:141-149)

Neither is a security boundary against a malicious command that passes the denylist.

## Parity reference
- CC: tools/BashTool/shouldUseSandbox.ts, utils/sandbox/sandbox-adapter.js
- OC: missing entirely

## Acceptance
- Linux: seccomp or firejail wrapping of bash -c
- macOS: sandbox-exec profile
- shouldUseSandbox() equivalent gating function
- dangerouslyDisableSandbox input field respected when policy allows
- User-configurable excludedCommands list
